 {"id":"process_triage-f5o","title":"Implement progress event emission system","description":"## Task
Implement the progress event system that reports collection stages to TUI and agent CLI.

## Background
Both TUI and agent CLI need to show progress:
- scan_started, scan_progress, scan_complete
- deep_scan_started, deep_scan_progress, deep_scan_complete
- infer_started, infer_complete
- decide_started, decide_complete
- plan_ready

Events enable:
- TUI progress visualization
- pt agent tail --format jsonl streaming
- Timeout detection (no events = hung)

## Event Structure
{
  "event": "scan_progress",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-15T14:30:22Z",
  "session_id": "abc123",
  "phase": "quick_scan",
  "progress": {"current": 150, "total": 312},
  "details": {"pids_scanned": 150}
}

## Implementation Notes
- Use channels for event dispatch
- Support multiple subscribers (TUI, file, network)
- Events should be cheap (not slow down collection)
- Include timing information for performance analysis

## Event Types
- session_started, session_ended
- quick_scan_{started,progress,complete}
- deep_scan_{started,progress,complete}
- inference_{started,progress,complete}
- decision_{started,complete}
- action_{started,complete,failed}
- plan_ready

## Deliverables
- Rust module: events/mod.rs
- Event type definitions
- Event emitter trait and implementations
- JSONL formatter for CLI
- Unit tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

 {"id":"process_triage-fh0d","title":"Implement EVT tail modeling (POT/GPD)","description":"## Overview
Implement Extreme Value Theory (EVT) with Peaks Over Threshold (POT) and Generalized Pareto Distribution (GPD) for modeling extreme spikes.

## From Plan Section 4.35

### Mathematical Foundation
Model exceedances of CPU/IO/network bursts above a high threshold u0 using GPD.

**Generalized Pareto Distribution**:
For exceedances X - u0 where X > u0:
```
P(X - u0 > y) = (1 + ξy/σ)^(-1/ξ)   for ξ ≠ 0
             = exp(-y/σ)             for ξ = 0
```

Where:
- ξ: Shape parameter (tail heaviness)
- σ: Scale parameter
- u0: Threshold

**Parameter Estimation**:
- Maximum Likelihood Estimation (MLE)
- Probability Weighted Moments (PWM)

### Use Cases
- Detect pathological CPU bursts vs normal spikes
- Model heavy-tail behavior in IO
- Quantify extreme network event probability
- Risk penalties (CVaR) for tail events

### Integration with Decision Core
- EVT provides tail probability estimates
- Heavy-tail evidence feeds into posterior computation
- Supports CVaR risk measure for risk-sensitive control

### Threshold Selection
- Mean residual life plot
- Parameter stability over threshold range
- Use automated selection with validation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] GPD distribution implemented
- [ ] MLE/PWM parameter estimation works
- [ ] Threshold selection method implemented
- [ ] Tail probability computation correct
- [ ] Integration with risk measures (CVaR)

## Dependencies
- Phase 3 (evidence collection for extreme events)
- Phase 4 (inference integration)

## Technical Notes
- Handle ξ ≈ 0 case carefully (exponential limit)
- Validate with goodness-of-fit tests
- Consider block maxima alternative for validation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:55:29.621582615Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T17:55:49.460073669Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T17:55:49.460073669Z","close_reason":"Implemented EVT tail modeling with GPD: GpdFitter with PWM/MLE estimation, TailType classification (light/exponential/heavy/very-heavy), risk measures (return_level, cvar, tail_probability), BatchEvtAnalyzer for multi-metric analysis. 20 tests passing.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-fh0d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-iau","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-fh0d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-nao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-fk7","title":"Implement macOS-specific collection","description":"## Task
Implement evidence collection for macOS platform.

## Background
macOS has no /proc filesystem and uses different tools:
- fs_usage: filesystem activity tracing
- sample/spindump: stack sampling
- nettop: network connections per process
- powermetrics: power and performance metrics
- lsof: file descriptors
- vm_stat: memory statistics

## Data Collection Approach
1. Quick scan: BSD ps (different flags than Linux)
2. Deep scan: lsof, netstat, sysctl
3. Maximal: sample, fs_usage (may require SIP disable)

## SIP Considerations
System Integrity Protection limits tracing:
- dtruss may not work
- Some tools require entitlements
- Detect SIP status and adjust capabilities

## Implementation Notes
- Platform detection at startup
- Separate collection modules per platform
- Abstract interface for cross-platform code
- Handle tool unavailability gracefully

## macOS-specific Features
- Activity Monitor integration info
- launchd service detection
- XPC service identification
- Spotlight/mds process handling

## Deliverables
- Rust module: collect/macos.rs
- Platform abstraction layer
- Unit tests with mocked macOS output
- Documentation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

-{"id":"process_triage-fqr","title":"Create packaging for all platforms","description":"## Overview
Create distribution packages for all target platforms.

## Background
The plan targets wide adoption, which requires easy installation on all major platforms. This means native packages, not just source builds.

## Why It Matters
Users expect apt install or brew install. Source builds are a barrier to adoption. Native packages enable one-command installation and system integration (man pages, shell completions).

## Platform Targets

### Linux
- deb package for Debian/Ubuntu
- rpm package for RHEL/Fedora
- AppImage for universal Linux
- Snap or Flatpak (optional)

### macOS
- Homebrew formula (primary)
- pkg installer for enterprise
- DMG with drag-to-Applications

### Windows
- MSI installer
- Chocolatey package
- Scoop manifest
- winget manifest

### Cross-platform
- Docker official image
- Cargo install from crates.io
- Pre-built binaries on GitHub releases

## Package Contents
- pt binary (or pt + pt-core)
- Man pages
- Shell completions (bash, zsh, fish)
- Example configuration files
- systemd unit file (Linux packages)
- launchd plist (macOS packages)

## Build Infrastructure
- Cross-compilation for all targets
- Automated package builds in CI
- Signing for authenticity (where applicable)
- Automated testing of packages

## Release Process
1. Tag release in git
2. CI builds all packages
3. Packages signed and uploaded
4. Package managers updated
5. Release notes published

## Success Criteria
- All platforms have working packages
- Installation is one command
- Packages include all components
- Updates work smoothly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Implements the full scope described above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Handles edge/error cases described above with deterministic behavior.
- [ ] Includes unit tests for the core logic and the highest-risk edge cases.
- [ ] Emits structured logs/telemetry for key decisions and failures (as applicable).

## Test Plan
- Unit: golden tests for core logic; edge cases and error paths.
- Integration: exercise real tool/system interactions where applicable (or mocked equivalents).
- E2E: cover at least one representative user/agent workflow when applicable.
- Logging: tests validate presence/shape of critical log/telemetry fields.

Validation run: bash -n + shellcheck + bats test/package_e2e.bats passed; UBS --diff executed (repo-size guard raised via UBS_MAX_DIR_SIZE_MB=5000) with no critical findings.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:38:22.582290777Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:24:13.759698748Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-fqr","depends_on_id":"process_triage-ica","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-fqr","depends_on_id":"process_triage-sj6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"process_triage-ftf8","title":"Implement streaming sketches (Count-Min, HyperLogLog, t-digest)","description":"## Section 4.10 - Streaming Sketches

**Purpose**: Memory-efficient approximate statistics for high-volume process telemetry. Cannot store all syscall counts or I/O events - need sublinear space algorithms.

**Mathematical Background**:
- Count-Min Sketch: d hash functions, w counters each. Query = min over rows. Space O(ε^-1 log(1/δ))
- HyperLogLog: Cardinality estimation via max leading zeros. Space O(ε^-2) bits
- t-digest: Quantile estimation via variable-width bins. Accurate at tails (p < 0.01, p > 0.99)
- Bloom filters: Membership testing with false positive rate ≈ (1-e^(-kn/m))^k

**Implementation Requirements**:
1. `CountMinSketch::new(epsilon, delta)` - Initialize with error/confidence
2. `CountMinSketch::update(item, count)` - Increment
3. `CountMinSketch::query(item)` - Get approximate count
4. `HyperLogLog::new(precision)` - p=14 gives ~1% error
5. `TDigest::new(compression)` - Higher = more accurate
6. `TDigest::quantile(p)` - Query percentile

**Why This Matters for pt**:
Long-running processes generate millions of events. We need O(KB) summaries, not O(GB) logs. t-digest lets us answer 'p99 syscall latency' without storing all latencies.

**Integration Points**:
- Evidence collection (Section 3.3)
- Runtime feature extraction (Section 4.3)
- Telemetry lake compression (Section 3.4)

**Test Requirements**:
- Verify error bounds hold empirically
- Verify merge operations work (critical for fleet aggregation)
- Benchmark memory usage vs accuracy","notes":"Duplicate/out-of-tree bead. Canonical implementation: process_triage-nao.5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:46:07.878086479Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:44.821429528Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:44.821429528Z","close_reason":"duplicate (canonical: process_triage-nao.5)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"process_triage-fvmq","title":"Implement fleet discovery and registration","description":"## Task: Fleet Discovery and Registration (Phase 14.1)

### Description
Implement host discovery and registration protocol for fleet coordination.

### Requirements
1. **Host Registration**
   ```json
   {
     "host_id": "dev-workstation-01",
     "hostname": "dev-workstation-01.local",
     "ip_addresses": ["192.168.1.100"],
     "capabilities": {
       "cores": 64,
       "memory_gb": 512,
       "pt_version": "2.1.0"
     },
     "registered_at": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
     "last_heartbeat": "2024-01-15T14:30:00Z",
     "status": "active"
   }
   ```

2. **Discovery Methods**
   - Manual: `pt fleet join --coordinator=host:port`
   - mDNS: Auto-discover hosts on local network
   - DNS-SD: Service discovery via DNS
   - Static config: List of known hosts in config file

3. **Heartbeat Protocol**
   ```
   Every 30s:
     Host → Coordinator: {host_id, timestamp, status, summary_stats}
     Coordinator → Host: {ack, fleet_status, pending_commands}
   
   Timeout: 3 missed heartbeats = host marked offline
   ```

4. **Security**
   - Shared secret for fleet authentication
   - TLS for inter-host communication
   - Host identity verification
   - Role-based access (coordinator vs member)

### CLI Interface
```bash
# Start as coordinator
pt fleet coordinator --bind=0.0.0.0:9876

# Join existing fleet
pt fleet join --coordinator=192.168.1.100:9876 --secret=xxx

# List fleet members
pt fleet members

# Leave fleet
pt fleet leave
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Hosts can join and leave fleet dynamically
- [ ] Heartbeat detects offline hosts within 2 minutes
- [ ] Authentication prevents unauthorized joins
- [ ] mDNS discovery works on local network","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:06:07.306457508Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-02T06:13:48.230281132Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T06:13:48.230134419Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-fvmq","depends_on_id":"process_triage-8t1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-fyr8","title":"Implement submodular probe selection for evidence gathering","description":"## Section 4.15 - Submodular Probe Selection

**Purpose**: Choose which expensive probes (strace, lsof, network inspection) to run for maximum information gain. Budget-constrained optimal sensing.

**Mathematical Background**:
- Submodularity: f(A ∪ {e}) - f(A) ≥ f(B ∪ {e}) - f(B) when A ⊆ B (diminishing returns)
- Greedy algorithm: Select e* = argmax_{e} [f(S ∪ {e}) - f(S)] / cost(e)
- Information gain: f(S) = H(Y) - H(Y|X_S) = mutual information
- Lazy evaluation: Skip elements that can't improve on current best
- Matroid constraint: At most k probes, or budget ≤ B

**Implementation Requirements**:
1. `information_gain(probe, current_evidence)` - Compute I(Y; X_probe | X_current)
2. `greedy_probe_selection(probes, budget)` - Budget-aware greedy
3. `lazy_greedy(probes, budget)` - Accelerated version
4. `probe_cost_model(probe)` - Time/resource cost estimates

**Why This Matters for pt**:
Deep scan has 10+ possible probes. Running all takes 30+ seconds. Submodular selection might achieve 90% of the information with 3 probes in 5 seconds.

**Integration Points**:
- Deep scan orchestration (Section 3.3)
- Active sensing (Section 5.4)
- Tool runner budget (Section 3.3.2)

**Test Requirements**:
- Verify greedy achieves (1-1/e) approximation guarantee
- Verify lazy evaluation correctness
- Benchmark probe selection vs exhaustive on real processes","notes":"Duplicate/out-of-tree bead. Canonical implementation: process_triage-p15.3.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:01.219000388Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:44.181468562Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T10:22:44.181468562Z","close_reason":"duplicate (canonical: process_triage-p15.3)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-fyr8","depends_on_id":"process_triage-cki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:30:58Z","created_by":"import"}]}
